problem: Polynomials $P(x)$ and $Q(x)$ each have degree $3$ and leading coefficient $1$, and their roots are all elements of $\{1,2,3,4,5\}$. The function $f(x) = \frac{P(x)}{Q(x)}$ has the property that there exist real numbers $a < b < c < d$ such that the set of all real $x$ with $f(x) \le 0$ consists of the closed interval $[a,b]$ together with the open interval $(c,d)$. How many functions $f(x)$ are possible?
